title: fraxinus pennsylvanica var. subintegerrima
intro: Fraxinus pennsylvanica var. subintegerrima, also known as Green Ash, is a deciduous tree native to eastern North America, characterized by its compound leaves and gray bark. The bark and leaves of the Green Ash plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it a valuable remedy for respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's medicinal properties also provide health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing sore throats, and alleviating digestive issues. Green Ash can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects.
